{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query states that complete denture teeth are in edge to edge contact in centric occlusion. This means that the teeth are touching each other when the jaw is in the resting position. To address this issue, the goal is to adjust the occlusion so that the teeth do not interfere with each other during centric occlusion. Option A suggests grinding the centric holding cusp, which would help to reduce the contact between the upper and lower teeth.
